Land Rover Range Rover P615 SV Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover P615 SV engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned P615 SV engine (S68B44B) typically costs £6,500–£10,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£3,000. A full replacement with labour can cost £9,000–£13,000. As a low‑volume, high‑performance engine, supply is limited – prices are indicative. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover P615 SV engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S68 is a BMW‑sourced 4.4‑litre twin‑turbo V8, representing a significant step up from JLR's previous in‑house V8s. Early reports suggest the newer S68 engines are improved over earlier BMW V8 designs. However, as a high‑output engine, it demands strict maintenance – regular oil changes (BMW LL‑spec) and premium fuel are essential. Reliability is a 'mixed bag' – some owners report flawless experiences, others have encountered issues.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover P615 SV?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common concerns include turbocharger wear, cooling system stresses due to high output, carbon buildup on intake valves, and potential timing chain wear (shared architecture with BMW's N63 family). Oil leaks from valve covers are also reported.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover P615 SV engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the P615 SV's extremely high value (typically £150,000–£250,000+), a £6,500–£12,000 reconditioned engine is a small fraction of the vehicle's worth. Replacement is almost always financially viable. Even at the higher end, it rarely exceeds our 60% Land Rover threshold against current market values.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P615 SV uses the BMW‑derived S68B44B 4.4‑litre twin‑turbo V8. This is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The P615 badge itself confirms the engine – it's the 615 PS V8 offered in the Range Rover SV.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair a P615 SV engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – turbocharger wear, cooling system stress, oil leaks – are repairable. However, major internal failure (crankshaft, bearings, catastrophic timing chain failure) would typically justify replacement over rebuild. The S68 is a complex engine requiring specialist knowledge.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover P615 SV Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs; warranty claims; full provenance

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current L460 P615 SV generation. As a low‑volume, high‑performance engine, supply of used and reconditioned units is limited — prices are indicative. Labour estimate: Fitting a Range Rover P615 SV engine typically takes 16–22 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent Land Rover specialist). A full replacement with labour can cost £9,000–£13,000.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P615 SV engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Turbocharger Wear / Cooling System Stress (High‑Output V8)
problems.symptoms: Turbo lag, unusual noises from the engine bay, coolant temperature fluctuations, loss of power, excessive exhaust smoke.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you notice power loss, unusual noises, or temperature fluctuations.

problems.recommendation: The BMW‑derived S68 is a high‑output twin‑turbo V8 producing 615 PS. High‑performance turbocharged engines place significant stress on the cooling system and turbochargers. Common issues may include turbocharger wear, carbon buildup on intake valves, and cooling system stresses. Regular oil changes (using the correct BMW LL‑spec oil) and allowing the engine to cool after hard driving are essential preventative measures. Monitor for turbo lag, unusual noises, and coolant temperature fluctuations.

problems.title: Timing Chain Wear (BMW S68 / N63 Family)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, poor engine performance, engine warning light, potential complete engine failure if the chain jumps or breaks.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: The BMW S68 shares architecture with the N63 family, which has a historical reputation for timing chain issues. A failing timing chain can cause rattling noises, poor engine performance, or even complete engine failure if it jumps on the sprockets or breaks. While the S68 is an improved design, it remains a high‑stress, high‑output engine. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, have the timing chain inspected immediately.

problems.title: Oil Leaks / Valve Cover Seepage (BMW V8 Architecture)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top or sides of the engine.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: BMW V8s are known for oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and other seals. Ignition failures or oil leaks left unresolved can affect other systems, reducing overall vehicle reliability and safety. Given the P615 SV's high‑value status, maintaining correct oil level is critical. If you notice oil spots under your Range Rover or a burning oil smell, investigate immediately.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover P615 SV — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Always worth fixing — overheating kills V8s
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover P615 SV? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Land Rover service history with correct BMW‑spec oil (LL‑04/LL‑17 FE+)
* Evidence of regular oil changes (every 5,000–8,000 miles) — critical for this engine
* Cooling system inspected and up to date
* Warranty remaining — the S68 is a complex, expensive engine to repair
avoidIf: * No evidence of regular oil changes — this engine is intolerant of neglect
* Any sign of timing chain rattle on cold start
* Poor service history — these engines demand strict maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover P615 SV represents the pinnacle of Range Rover performance — the most powerful series‑production Range Rover ever built, powered by a BMW‑sourced 4.4‑litre twin‑turbo V8 with 615 PS. However, high performance comes with high maintenance demands. The S68 engine is a sophisticated, high‑stress unit that demands strict servicing and premium fuel. Buyers should prioritise vehicles with full service history and remaining warranty. When maintained correctly, it's a genuinely remarkable engine; neglected examples can be ruinously expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P615 SV engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover P615 SV (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any rattle from the front of the engine? (timing chain warning)
2. Service History — Full history with BMW‑spec oil — this is non‑negotiable
3. Turbo Health — Any whistling, lag, or smoke on acceleration?
4. Coolant Level — Any sign of coolant loss? (cooling system stress is common)
5. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the valve covers
6. Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any turbo or timing codes?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
